title: Team-aware pipeline-shape selection
description: Resolve the pipeline engine / encoded SOP from the active team's pipelineShape instead of solely from global config. The programming team continues to select exactly what selectPipelineEngine(config) selects today; a team declaring a different pipelineShape selects the corresponding engine. The existing eligibility and 'careful'-mode downgrade rules still apply. runPipeline resolves the active team and uses its shape; with no team specified it resolves to 'programming' and behaves identically to today.

successCriteria.description: The project builds successfully with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build).
TypeScript compilation produces zero type errors in strict mode (npm run typecheck).
All tests pass with zero failures, including new team-aware selection tests and the existing selector/eligibility/workflow-engine tests (npm run test).

For the programming team, the selected engine is identical to selectPipelineEngine(config) today. A unit test asserts the programming team with engine 'ts' selects a TsPipelineEngine (same instance type as the legacy path).

A team declaring a different pipelineShape selects the corresponding engine. A unit test builds a team with pipelineShape 'workflow' (eligible config) and asserts a WorkflowEngine is selected, and that an ineligible/'careful' config still downgrades to TS (eligibility rules preserved).

runPipeline resolves the active team (loadTeam) and uses its pipelineShape; called with no team it resolves to 'programming' and behaves as before. A unit/integration test (with a stubbed engine) asserts the team's shape drives engine selection and the no-team path is unchanged.

nonGoals: Do not implement a new pipeline engine or a medical SOP — only resolve the EXISTING engines ('ts'|'skill'|'workflow') from the team's shape
Do not change memory namespacing — that was Sprint 2
Do not add CLI --team flags or the example team — that is Sprint 4
Do not change eligibility or 'careful'-mode downgrade semantics
stopConditions: Programming team selects today's engine; a team declaring a different shape selects the corresponding engine with eligibility rules intact
definitionOfDone: A team-aware shape resolver selects the PipelineEngine from the active team's pipelineShape, reusing resolveEngineName/selectPipelineEngine semantics (eligibility + careful downgrade preserved). runPipeline resolves the active team via loadTeam and uses its shape; the no-team/programming path is byte-for-byte today's behavior. Build, typecheck, and all tests (new + existing selector/eligibility/workflow tests) pass.
assumptions: pipelineShape uses the existing PipelineEngineName union ('ts'|'skill'|'workflow'); 'skill' still falls through to TS as it does today.

Eligibility (isWorkflowEligible) and 'careful'-mode downgrade are evaluated against config as today; the team's shape only chooses the REQUESTED engine name, then the existing downgrade logic applies.
How the active team id reaches runPipeline (an opts field or config.defaultTeam) is a generator decision; the no-team default must be 'programming'.
generatorNotes:
Reuse, do not rewrite, the selector.

Verified touch points:
- src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts: resolveEngineName(config):PipelineEngineName at :22 and selectPipelineEngine(config):PipelineEngine at :50. Add a team-aware variant — e.g. selectPipelineEngineForTeam(team, config) or extend resolveEngineName to take an optional requested-engine override from team.pipelineShape — that uses team.pipelineShape as the REQUESTED engine name, then applies the SAME downgrade pipeline (isWorkflowEligible + config.pipeline.mode==='careful' => 'ts'). Keep selectPipelineEngine(config) working for back-compat (it can delegate to the team-aware path with the programming team).
- src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts: runPipeline at :970 calls selectPipelineEngine(config) at :977. Resolve the active team (loadTeam(config, teamId)) and pass team.pipelineShape into the team-aware selector. Determine teamId from opts (preferred, additive) or config.defaultTeam; default 'programming'.
- src/orchestrator/workflow/engine.ts: PipelineEngine / PipelineEngineName.
- Eligibility: src/orchestrator/workflow/eligibility.ts (isWorkflowEligible).

Programming team: team.pipelineShape === resolveEngineName(config) (set in Sprint 1), so the programming path is identical to today by construction. Verify this equivalence explicitly in a test.

Keep the change minimal and additive; do not alter the downgrade log lines or eligibility semantics. Collocate tests in selector.test.ts (extend) and a runPipeline-level test using a stubbed/injected engine factory so no real LLM calls happen.
evaluatorNotes:
sc-3-4: build the programming team (loadTeam(config)); team-aware selector returns the same engine class as selectPipelineEngine(config) for engine 'ts' (TsPipelineEngine).
sc-3-5: team with pipelineShape 'workflow' + an eligible config -> WorkflowEngine; same shape with an ineligible config OR mode 'careful' -> TsPipelineEngine (downgrade preserved, one log line as before).
sc-3-6: with a stubbed engine factory, assert runPipeline picks the engine matching the active team's shape; no-team call uses programming and matches the legacy selection.
Regression: run existing selector/eligibility/workflow-engine tests (must stay green). Run npm run build, npm run typecheck, npm run test.
